name: BELLE
narrative: What would become a devastating major hurricane first appeared as a tropical disturbance over the western Atlantic on August 6, 1976 — Hurricane Belle was born. It muscled its way to Category 3 hurricane intensity on August 9, with maximum sustained winds reaching 120 mph (105 kt). Central pressure dropped to 957 mb at the storm's peak. The storm remained over open water, threatening only shipping lanes. The system lasted approximately 4 days. By August 10, the system had unraveled over the western Atlantic off the U.S. East Coast. Notable: Underwent rapid intensification (winds increased ~51 mph in 24 hours)
